devanagari: रैवतके द्विविद्वधः । श्रीराजोवाच । भूयोऽहं श्रोतुमिच्छामि रामस्याद्भुतकर्मणः । अनन्तस्याप्रमेयस्य यदन्यत् कृतवान् प्रभुः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: raivatake dvividvadhaḥśrīrājovācabhūyo'haṃ śrotumicchāmi rāmasyādbhutakarmaṇaḥanantasyāprameyasya yadanyat kṛtavān prabhuḥ
translation:
King Parīkṣit said:
I long to hear again of the wonderful deeds of Balarāma, the possessor of infinite, immeasurable power and miraculous feats. Please narrate what other wondrous deeds he performed.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । नरकस्य सखा कश्चिद् द्विविदो नाम वानरः । सुग्रीवसचिवः सोऽथ भ्राता मैन्दस्य वीर्यवान् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācanarakasya sakhā kaścid dvivido nāma vānaraḥsugrīvasacivaḥ so'tha bhrātā maindasya vīryavān
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
There was a monkey called Dvivida, a friend of Narakāsura. He was a counsellor of Sugrīva and a brave and powerful brother of Mainda.

devanagari: सख्युः सोऽपचितिं कुर्वन् वानरो राष्ट्रविप्लवम् । पुरग्रामाकरान् घोषान् अदहद् वह्निमुत्सृजन् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: sakhyuḥ so'pacitiṃ kurvan vānaro rāṣṭraviplavampuragrāmākarān ghoṣān adahad vahnimutsṛjan
translation: In order to avenge the death of his friend Naraka, he caused a great havoc in the country by setting on fire cities, villages, mines and cowherds’ camps.

devanagari: क्वचित् स शैलानुत्पाट्य तैर्देशान् समचूर्णयत् । आनर्तान् सुतरामेव यत्रास्ते मित्रहा हरिः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: kvacit sa śailānutpāṭya tairdeśān samacūrṇayatānartān sutarāmeva yatrāste mitrahā hariḥ
translation: Sometimes he uprooted the hills and completely reduced to powder the whole of the Ānarta[1] country where Hari, the slayer of his friend, was living.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Western Gujarat with Dvārakā as its capital.

devanagari: क्वचित् समुद्रमध्यस्थो दोर्भ्यां उत्क्षिप्य तज्जलम् । देशान् नागायुतप्राणो वेलाकूले न्यमज्जयत् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: kvacit samudramadhyastho dorbhyāṃ utkṣipya tajjalamdeśān nāgāyutaprāṇo velākūle nyamajjayat
translation: He was possessed of the strength of ten thousand elephants. Some times he used to stand in the midst of the sea and create a high tide by dashing up volume of seawater with his arms and drown the coastal area under water.

devanagari: आश्रमान् ऋषिमुख्यानां कृत्वा भग्नवनस्पतीन् । अदूषयत् शकृत् मूत्रैः अग्नीन् वैतानिकान् खलः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: āśramān ṛṣimukhyānāṃ kṛtvā bhagnavanaspatīnadūṣayat śakṛt mūtraiḥ agnīn vaitānikān khalaḥ
translation: The wicked fellow broke down big trees in the hermitages of the great sages and desecrated the sacrificial fires with urine and excreta.

devanagari: पुरुषान् योषितो दृप्तः क्ष्माभृद्द्रोणीगुहासु सः । निक्षिप्य चाप्यधाच्छैलैः पेशस्क्कास्कारीव कीटकम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: puruṣān yoṣito dṛptaḥ kṣmābhṛddroṇīguhāsu saḥnikṣipya cāpyadhācchailaiḥ peśaskkāskārīva kīṭakam
translation: Like a wasp imprisoning a worm the naughty monkey shut up men and women in the valleys and caves of mountains and blocked their entrance with big stones.

devanagari: एवं देशान् विप्रकुर्वन् दूषयंश्च कुलस्त्रियः । श्रुत्वा सुललितं गीतं गिरिं रैवतकं ययौ ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ deśān viprakurvan dūṣayaṃśca kulastriyaḥśrutvā sulalitaṃ gītaṃ giriṃ raivatakaṃ yayau
translation: In this way he wrought havock in the country and ravished women of noble birth. Once, hearing sweet enchanting music, he went to the Raivataka hill.

devanagari: तत्रापश्यद् यदुपतिं रामं पुष्करमालिनम् । सुदर्शनीयसर्वाङ्गं ललनायूथमध्यगम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: tatrāpaśyad yadupatiṃ rāmaṃ puṣkaramālinamsudarśanīyasarvāṅgaṃ lalanāyūthamadhyagam
translation: There he espied Balarāma, the Lord of Yadus, wearing a lotus-garland, charming in every limb, standing in the midst of a bevy of beautiful damsels.

devanagari: गायन्तं वारुणीं पीत्वा मदविह्वललोचनम् । विभ्राजमानं वपुषा प्रभिन्नमिव वारणम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: gāyantaṃ vāruṇīṃ pītvā madavihvalalocanamvibhrājamānaṃ vapuṣā prabhinnamiva vāraṇam
translation: While singing he was drunk with Vāruṇī and his eyes were swimming with intoxication. He had a brilliant personality and looked like an elephant exuding ichor.

devanagari: दुष्टः शाखामृगः शाखां आरूढः कंपयन् द्रुमान् । चक्रे किलकिलाशब्दं आत्मानं संप्रदर्शयन् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: duṣṭaḥ śākhāmṛgaḥ śākhāṃ ārūḍhaḥ kaṃpayan drumāncakre kilakilāśabdaṃ ātmānaṃ saṃpradarśayan
translation: The wicked monkey climbed the boughs of the trees and shaking the trees heavily made a chattering sound to attract their notice to his presence.

devanagari: तस्य धार्ष्ट्यं कपेर्वीक्ष्य तरुण्यो जातिचापलाः । हास्यप्रिया विजहसुः बलदेवपरिग्रहाः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya dhārṣṭyaṃ kapervīkṣya taruṇyo jāticāpalāḥhāsyapriyā vijahasuḥ baladevaparigrahāḥ
translation: Beholding the insolence of the monkey those young damsels, the spouses of Balarāma, being fickle by nature and fond of fun, burst out into a heavy laughter.

devanagari: ता हेलयामास कपिः भूक्षेपैः संमुखादिभिः । दर्शयन् स्वगुदं तासां रामस्य च निरीक्षितः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: tā helayāmāsa kapiḥ bhūkṣepaiḥ saṃmukhādibhiḥdarśayan svagudaṃ tāsāṃ rāmasya ca nirīkṣitaḥ
translation: In the teeth of Balarāma’s presence, the monkey began to insult the damsels by twisting his eyebrows, making wry faces at them and showing them his hind part. Balarāma, the foremost among warriors, got enraged and hurled a big stone at the monkey.

devanagari: तं ग्राव्णा प्राहरत् क्रुद्धो बलः प्रहरतां वरः । स वञ्चयित्वा ग्रावाणं मदिराकलशं कपिः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ grāvṇā prāharat kruddho balaḥ praharatāṃ varaḥsa vañcayitvā grāvāṇaṃ madirākalaśaṃ kapiḥ
translation: But that wily monkey evaded the stone and (swooping down from the trees) took away the pitcher of wine (from Balarāma) and ridiculed Balarāma by laughing loudly and provoking Balarāma’s wrath.

devanagari: गृहीत्वा हेलयामास धूर्तस्तं कोपयन् हसन् । निर्भिद्य कलशं दुष्टो वासांस्यास्फालयद् बलम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: gṛhītvā helayāmāsa dhūrtastaṃ kopayan hasannirbhidya kalaśaṃ duṣṭo vāsāṃsyāsphālayad balam
translation: The wicked monkey dashed down the pitcher of wine, tore the clothes of women. Being puffed up with pride, he treated Balarāma with utmost contempt and caused him excessive annoyance.

devanagari: कदर्थीकृत्य बलवान् विप्रचक्रे मदोद्धतः । तं तस्याविनयं दृष्ट्वा देशांश्च तदुपद्रुतान् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: kadarthīkṛtya balavān vipracakre madoddhataḥtaṃ tasyāvinayaṃ dṛṣṭvā deśāṃśca tadupadrutān
translation: Seeing his mischievous and arrogant behaviour and considering the havoc caused by him in the country, Balarāma flew in rage and took up his pestle and plough in hand, in order to kill the enemy.

devanagari: क्रुद्धो मुसलमादत्त हलं चारिजिघांसया । द्विविदोऽपि महावीर्यः शालमुद्यम्य पाणिना ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: kruddho musalamādatta halaṃ cārijighāṃsayādvivido'pi mahāvīryaḥ śālamudyamya pāṇinā
translation: Dvivida too was endowed with great strength. He uprooted a Śāla tree with his hand and rushing with great vehemence at Balarāma, struck his head with the tree.

devanagari: अभ्येत्य तरसा तेन बलं मूर्धन्यताडयत् । तं तु सङ्कर्षणो मूर्ध्नि पतन्तमचलो यथा ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: abhyetya tarasā tena balaṃ mūrdhanyatāḍayattaṃ tu saṅkarṣaṇo mūrdhni patantamacalo yathā
translation: The mighty Balarāma stood firm like a mountain. He caught hold of the tree (as its blow was) falling on his head and hit Dvivida with the pestle called Sunanda.

devanagari: प्रतिजग्राह बलवान् सुनन्देनाहनच्च तम् । मूषलाहतमस्तिष्को विरेजे रक्तधारया ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: pratijagrāha balavān sunandenāhanacca tammūṣalāhatamastiṣko vireje raktadhārayā
translation: With his head crushed with the pestle, it became covered with a stream of blood. Dviviḍa shone with the torrent of blood like a mountain covered with a flow of liquid red chalk. In spite of that, he did not mind Balarāma’s stroke.

devanagari: गिरिर्यथा गैरिकया प्रहारं नानुचिन्तयन् । पुनरन्यं समुत्क्षिप्य कृत्वा निष्पत्रमोजसा ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: giriryathā gairikayā prahāraṃ nānucintayanpunaranyaṃ samutkṣipya kṛtvā niṣpatramojasā
translation: He again pulled out another palm tree and removing all its leaves by dint of his physical power, he hit Balarāma in rage. But Balarāma splintered it into hundred pieces.

devanagari: तेनाहनत् सुसङ्क्रुद्धः तं बलः शतधाच्छिनत् । ततोऽन्येन रुषा जघ्ने तं चापि शतधाच्छिनत् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: tenāhanat susaṅkruddhaḥ taṃ balaḥ śatadhācchinattato'nyena ruṣā jaghne taṃ cāpi śatadhācchinat
translation: The monkey again struck him down with another palm tree which Balarāma cut into hundreds of pieces. Fighting in this way with the glorious Lord, he pulled out a palm tree to strike which was broken to hundreds of pieces. Uprooting trees on all sides, he denuded the forest into a treeless tract.

devanagari: एवं युध्यन् भगवता भग्ने भग्ने पुनः पुनः । आकृष्य सर्वतो वृक्षान् निर्वृक्षं अकरोद् वनम् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ yudhyan bhagavatā bhagne bhagne punaḥ punaḥākṛṣya sarvato vṛkṣān nirvṛkṣaṃ akarod vanam
translation: The monkey again struck him down with another palm tree which Balarāma cut into hundreds of pieces. Fighting in this way with the glorious Lord, he pulled out a palm tree to strike which was broken to hundreds of pieces. Uprooting trees on all sides, he denuded the forest into a treeless tract.

devanagari: ततोऽमुञ्चच्छिलावर्षं बलस्योपर्यमर्षितः । तत्सर्वं चूर्णयामास लीलया मुसलायुधः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: tato'muñcacchilāvarṣaṃ balasyoparyamarṣitaḥtatsarvaṃ cūrṇayāmāsa līlayā musalāyudhaḥ
translation: Being enraged, he discharged a shower of rocks on Balarāma all of which the wielḍer of pestle as his weapon, reduced to powder.

devanagari: स बाहू तालसङ्काशौ मुष्टीकृत्य कपीश्वरः । आसाद्य रोहिणीपुत्रं ताभ्यां वक्षस्यरूरुजत् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: sa bāhū tālasaṅkāśau muṣṭīkṛtya kapīśvaraḥāsādya rohiṇīputraṃ tābhyāṃ vakṣasyarūrujat
translation: The leader of monkeys clenched his palm-like long arms into fists and approaching the son of Rohiṇī (Balarāma), he hit his chest with both the fists.

devanagari: यादवेन्द्रोऽपि तं दोर्भ्यां त्यक्त्वा मुसललाङ्गले । जत्रावभ्यर्दयत्क्रुद्धः सोऽपतद् रुधिरं वमन् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: yādavendro'pi taṃ dorbhyāṃ tyaktvā musalalāṅgalejatrāvabhyardayatkruddhaḥ so'patad rudhiraṃ vaman
translation: The ruler of Yādavas also threw down his pestle and plough and angrily hit with both of his hands on his (Dvivida’s) collar-bones. And he fell down vomitting blood.

devanagari: चकम्पे तेन पतता सटङ्कः सवनस्पतिः । पर्वतः कुरुशार्दूल वायुना नौरिवाम्भसि ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: cakampe tena patatā saṭaṅkaḥ savanaspatiḥparvataḥ kuruśārdūla vāyunā naurivāmbhasi
translation: O king Parīkṣit (a tiger among the Kurus)! As Dvivida fell down, the whole of the mountain along with its peaks and trees began to shake like a boat tossed with a tempest in the sea water.

devanagari: जयशब्दो नमःशब्दः साधु साध्विति चाम्बरे । सुरसिद्धमुनीन्द्राणां आसीत् कुसुमवर्षिणाम् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: jayaśabdo namaḥśabdaḥ sādhu sādhviti cāmbaresurasiddhamunīndrāṇāṃ āsīt kusumavarṣiṇām
translation: There arose in the skies shouts of victory, salutation and approbation: ‘Well-done’, ‘Well-done’, from gods, Siddhas, prominent sages who were sending forth showers of flowers.

devanagari: एवं निहत्य द्विविदं जगद्व्यतिकरावहम् । संस्तूयमानो भगवान् जनैः स्वपुरमाविशत् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ nihatya dvividaṃ jagadvyatikarāvahamsaṃstūyamāno bhagavān janaiḥ svapuramāviśat
translation:
Killing in this way Dvivida, the scourage of the land (rendering it desolate), the glorious Lord Balarāma entered his city while he was being glorified in songs by the citizens.
[back to top]

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे उत्तरार्धे । द्विविदवधो नाम सप्तषष्टितमोऽध्यायः ॥ ६७ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃsaṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe uttarārdhedvividavadho nāma saptaṣaṣṭitamo'dhyāyaḥ
